title: An Artificial Sun
summary: Night light engineered to feel like daylight, shaped by the city.

photos.title: Vanishing Grid
photos.note: In IMG_2902, I shot close along the tiled wall so the repeating squares collapse into a strong vanishing point. The shallow angle, cool blue cast, and deep shadows turn a plain surface into something architectural and tense.

photos.title: Single Orbit
photos.note: In IMG_2767, I isolated the ceiling light against a flat grey background, using negative space and a diagonal line to make the small fixture feel graphic. The centered lamp becomes the anchor, while the rail cuts the frame with clean geometric tension.

photos.title: Quiet Apparatus
photos.note: In IMG_2901, I framed the bedroom with the tripod, fan, curtains, and rumpled bed acting like still-life elements. The dim exposure and cold tones make the room feel observational, while the vertical tripod adds a strange human absence.

photos.title: Window Witness
photos.note: In IMG_2766, I photographed through the window at night, using the frame and curtains as boundaries around the street scene outside. The contrast between the bright interior whites and warm exterior lights creates separation, distance, and a watched-from-inside feeling.

photos.title: Floorline Drift
photos.note: In IMG_2761, I placed the camera very low to the floor, letting the foreground blur and reflected light dominate the image. The unusual viewpoint turns the kitchen into a wide, quiet landscape, with chairs and cabinets reduced to silhouettes.

photos.title: Metal Tangle
photos.note: In IMG_2898, I moved in close on the pile of cutlery, using hard flash-like light to catch scratches, curves, and reflections. The overlapping forks, knives, and spoons create controlled visual clutter, making ordinary utensils feel sharp and sculptural.

photos.title: Industrial Halo
photos.note: In IMG_2764, I tilted the camera upward toward the range hood and tile corner, using diagonals to make the kitchen feel compressed and mechanical. The small light, gridded wall, and metal filter create a cold, industrial composition built from texture and angles.

title: Intermission
summary: Brief pauses between movement, where posture and light take over.

photos.title: Winter Amber
photos.note: The sun dissolves into the background, becoming the subject itself, while dense foreground layers frame a quiet, almost enclosed piece of nature.

photos.title: Suburban Mist
photos.note: Steam catches the low sun, turning an ordinary suburban scene into something fleeting and cinematic, with tree silhouettes cutting sharply through the haze.

photos.title: Downtown Davos
photos.note: A stitched perspective compresses the town into a dense, almost overwhelming landscape, blurring the line between observation and scale.

photos.title: Monochromatic Valley
photos.note: A restrained palette flattens the scene, while a single leading line quietly pulls the eye through the valley, softened by snow that mimics the texture of film grain.

photos.title: Storm on Piste
photos.note: A lone figure dissolves into the haze, where imbalance and negative space amplify the sense of isolation against the storm.

photos.title: Fresh smell of Pine
photos.note: Framed through a natural arch, the scene reveals itself in layers-snow, shadow, and filtered light-like a brief glimpse beyond a barrier.

photos.title: Miles of Alps
photos.note: The scale stretches beyond comprehension, inviting a quiet question: how far could you go before the landscape swallows you whole?

photos.title: Rapid Descent
photos.note: A fleeting moment of motion-captured mid-descent-where diagonal energy and centered framing collide to heighten the sense of speed.

photos.title: The Warmest Cold Day
photos.note: Cold concrete tones anchor the frame, allowing the warmth of the sky to breathe-resisting over-processing keeps the moment honest.

title: Between the Trees
summary: Quiet density, winter air, and the space the light leaves behind.

photos.title: Up
photos.note: Ultrawide perspective aimed into the sky, exaggerating the height of the trees. Contrast takes priority over color.

photos.title: Biosphere
photos.note: Shot at minimal focus distance. Fine plant hairs and droplet magnification reveal intricate detail within an otherwise dim, enclosed space.

photos.title: Liquid Glass
photos.note: Focusing on the tree reflection rather than the puddle itself creates a more introspective mood.

photos.title: Frost Creek
photos.note: A wide angle with the creek framed off-center. The composition divides horizontally into thirds: leaves, dark mud, and ice.

photos.title: Tiny Stream
photos.note: Shot at close range for the shallowest depth of field. In post, a subtle golden fade enhances the warmth.

title: Pocket Archeology
summary: Found fragments and small histories, collected with patient light.

photos.title: Confiscated Motion
photos.note: I froze the empty phone case mid-throw with a high shutter speed, keeping the background soft so the shape feels suspended and slightly unreal. The missing phone matters: it points directly to the school rule, turning absence into the subject.

photos.title: Carried Reflection
photos.note: I moved in close on the reflective Apple logo of the MacBook I always keep in my bag, letting the polished surface catch light like a small mirror. The tight framing makes a familiar object feel personal, quiet, and almost hidden.

photos.title: Measured Still Life
photos.note: I arranged the stationery with strict spacing and let the diagonal shadow divide the frame, using warm floor tones and minimal composition to make the objects feel deliberate and quiet.

photos.title: Binder Spine
photos.note: I treated the binder as a close-detail study, using the shallow angle, repeated lines, and saturated edge colors to make something practical feel graphic. The framing focuses on texture and structure rather than the whole object, making the binder read like part of the school day's architecture.

photos.title: Teeth of the Ordinary
photos.note: I pushed in close with a shallow focal plane so the zipper teeth become macro-like, using the cool blue fabric and hard diagonal framing to turn a common object into something mechanical and precise.

photos.title: Glass Current
photos.note: Shot low through the bottle, the refracted water bends the background into motion, while the bright patio light and clean cyan tones make the pour feel suspended and sculptural.

photos.title: Beacon in Noon Light
photos.note: The low angle exaggerates the bottle into a tower, with the sun flare and deep blue sky giving the simple form a bold, almost monumental presence.

photos.title: Edge of Glare
photos.note: I framed directly into the sun from beneath the object, allowing lens flare and shadow to dominate, because the image is less about the subject and more about the collision of light and form.

photos.title: Small Stage
photos.note: The bottle and folded ruler are placed against the railing like props, with the blossoms blurred behind them and the hard shadow grounding the scene in bright afternoon clarity.

photos.title: Shadow Notes
photos.note: I exposed for the sunlit deck so the objects disappear into graphic silhouettes, letting their shapes read like marks on paper: simple, abstract, and sharply timed.
